observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary
adjusting entries to accrue interest incurred during the year and capitalize
borrowing costs directly attributable to the construction of qualifying assets, in
accordance with IPSAS 1—Presentation of Financial Statements and IPSAS 5—
Borrowing Costs.

We recommended that the Municipal General Services Officer or other
appropriate officials assess the recoverable service amount of unserviceable
properties and all other assets with indications of impairment and coordinate
with the Municipal Accountant to recognize impairment losses, if warranted, in
accordance with IPSAS 21.

We recommended that the:
a. MDRRMC and LDC explain the non-inclusion of PPAs funded by BGCM and LDRRMF in the LDRRMFIP and/or SIP;
b. Local Finance Committee ensure that (i) prescribed budgeting process is religiously followed; (ii) all PPAs were included in the AIP/SIP and in the LDRRMFIP for PPAs under DRRM; and (iii) the expense class is explicitly indicated in the Appropriation Ordinance based on the AIP/SIP; and
c. Municipal Accountant cause the reversion of the amount of ₱864,050.00 from STF to General Fund and make it continuing until the corresponding PPAs funded therefor are already completed and any savings be made available for use subject to applicable budgeting and accounting regulations.

We recommended that the BAC, end-user and other concerned officials:
a. provide justification on the noted inconsistencies in the mode of
procurements indicated in various documents explaining, among others,
the actual mode undertaken for each of the subject procurement project
and the various noted non-compliance to GPPB Circular No. 2020-01 for
Emergency Procurement under Bayanihan Act;
b. henceforth, ensure that information and details in the supporting
documents, such as APP/PPMP (including approved revisions thereto, if
any), POs and BAC Resolutions, are properly and clearly indicated with
consistency;
c. ensure that NOA, PO/Contract and NTP, if necessary, are posted in
PhilGEPS/GPPB Online Portal or any other online platforms that may be
required for the purpose and likewise consistently attach proof of posting
to the LGU’s website and conspicuous places within the Municipality;
and
d. submit proof showing the actual number and composition of goods
distributed during the implementation of community quarantine and the
duly acknowledged proof of distribution of the 5,000 bags of relief goodstotaling ₱2,957,589.29.

We recommended that the
a. MBO (i) submit explanation in allowing charges of travelling expenses of
personnel who fetched the LSIs from Calapan City even if such activity
was not specifically provided in the AO nor covered with the
Revised/Supplemental LDRRMFIP, (ii) evaluate the accuracy of posting
of the obligation for procurement of acrylic barrier and coordinate with
the Municipal Accountant for proper adjustment in the amount to be
made continuing in the GF and transferred to STF, (iii) expedite the
utilization of the eBudget system to facilitate preparation of the SAAOB;
and (iv) ensure that appropriation is classified into appropriate expense
class and obligations are posted into proper appropriation under specific
PPAs; and
b. Municipal Accountant (i) submit justification on the incurrence of
obligation in excess/absence of appropriation for such specific purpose
under Special Trust Fund, and (ii) devise a system of monitoring of
unexpended balance of LDRRMF under STF per approved PPA to avoid
overcharging of expenses and/or payment of expenses without
corresponding approved appropriation.

We recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer,
Municipal Accountant and the concerned Department Heads submit the copy of
the approved DTRs and/or accomplishment reports of the 35 municipal
employees/JO personnel for audit purposes and ensure that future claims of the
same and other benefits are supported with all necessary documentation.

We recommended that the Local Finance Committee cause the posting of
the required utilization reports for the months of June to November 2020 and
other succeeding monthly reports in the Municipality’s website and to three (3)
conspicuous public places in the locality until the fund is fully liquidated.
Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor send the required
written notice to the DBM, House of Representatives, Senate of the Philippines,
House Committee on Appropriations, Senate Committee on Finance, and other
offices where the submission of reports is required under existing laws, rules,
and regulations when said reports have been posted on its website.

We recommended that the:
a. GSO (i) maintain Local Road Inventory and Road Map, (ii) prepare
RLRN with complete information per road network and (iii) assign Road
Network ID/PIN for each road name instead of procurement project to
facilitate monitoring of repairs and maintenance over the particular road
network;
b. Municipal Engineer (i) provide the Municipal Accountant and GSO with
the complete description and cost segregation of road components
included in the RLRN, and (ii) provide assistance to GSO in the
preparation and updating of Road Map;
c. Inventory Committee complete necessary information in the RPCLRN
and provide for the actual condition of the roads as at date of inventory
taking;
d. The Municipal Assessor and MPDC verify any third party claims and
other encumbrances on the road lots; and
e. Municipal Accountant (i) update and complete information in the
LRNLC in conformity with COA Circular No. 2015-008, (ii) include in
the Notes to Financial Statements the required disclosure for Road
Networks, (iii) evaluate the control of Municipality over road lots and
recognize the same in accordance with IPSAS 17; (iv) coordinate with the
Municipal Engineer and other concerned offices to ensure that replaced
parts of the road network were derecognized for any betterments and
replacements made therefor; and (v) exert efforts to retrieve documents
to identify details of unrecorded Public Infrastructure Assets totaling
₱1.7 million and draw necessary adjustments, if warranted.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to prepare the list
of unclaimed checks at the end of the year and submit the same to the Municipal
Accountant for preparation of JEV to restore the amount of unreleased checks
to Cash-in-Bank and recognize the appropriate liability account every end of
year, and reverse the same in the ensuing year.

We recommended that the:
a. BAC, its Secretariat and TWG
(i) provide justification on the extent of post-qualification conducted
for procurement projects that have Post-Qualification Report
prepared simultaneously with the Bid Evaluation Report and delay
in the posting of NOA and NTP in PhilGEPS;
(ii) ensure compliance with Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the Revised
IRR of RA 9184 in posting the NOA and NTP; and
(iii) conduct detailed post-qualification procedures as prescribed in Rule
X of the Revised IRR;
b. Municipal Engineer:
(i) ensure that as built plan prepared by the contractors are properly
inspected and evaluated and subsequently approved by the Head of
Agency;
(ii) prepare statement of time elapsed for every project’s progress
billing so that the details regarding the status of implementation of
the project for each progress billing could be determined; and
(iii) provide explanation for the noted deficiencies in the project
implementation and henceforth, ensure that projects were properly
implemented, inspected and evaluated;
c. Municipal Engineer and Accountant evaluate if delay was incurred for
the construction of Multi-Purpose Building cum evacuation Center in
barangay Maragooc and demand for liquidated damages, if any; and
d. Municipal Accountant ensure that future claims be supported with
complete documentation as required by the pertinent provisions of RA
9184 and its Revised IRR and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the:
a. MDRRMO (i) prepare and submit the Annual/Supplemental/Revised
LDRRMFIP to the LDRRMC for approval, which is subsequently
submitted by the latter to the LDC for inclusion in the
Annual/Supplemental IP, subject for approval of the Local Chief
Executive and the Local Sanggunian; and (ii) review and update the
LDRRMFIP annually to include the disaster activities to be funded from
the unexpended balance of QRF and MOOE from previous years that
were transferred in the STF;
b. LDC properly integrate the PPAs included in the LDRRMFIP into the
AIP, otherwise, require the MDRRMO to revised the plan in accordance
with the recommendations of the Council;
c. MPDC, MBO and MTO, as part of the Local Finance Committee, ensure
that the DRRM projects included in the annual budget/supplemental
budget are included in the Annual and Supplemental Investment Plan;
and
d. MDRRMO and Municipal Accountant ensure the correctness of
RSUDRRMF for proper monitoring of the budget, utilization and
balances of LDRRMF.

We recommended that the:
a. BAC (i) send invitations to observers at least five (5) calendar days before
the date of the procurement stage/activity, (ii) attach copy of written
notification sent to the losing bidder within three (3) days from the
issuance of the resolution recommending award of the contract, (iii) post
76
the Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed in the PhilGEPS within three
(3) calendar days from its issuance, (iv) consistently submit proof of
posting in any conspicuous place in the premises of the Municipality, (v)
submit justification on awarding contracts despite non-posting of proper
Performance Security for the supply and delivery of tools and equipment
for the establishment of common service facility for engineered bamboo
furniture and handicrafts (Lot 2) and henceforth, ensure that the winning
bidder post performance security in the prescribed form and amount, (vi)
properly accomplish the Abstract of Bids, (vii) make sure that the all
reports are dated, and (viii) be cautious in the preparation of the
applicable bidding documents, all in compliance to the Revised IRR of
RA 9184;
b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer secure valid official
receipts/invoices from the suppliers of the above infrastructure project to
properly substantiate payment made by the Municipality;
c. Municipal Mayor (i) grant extension not longer than the initial delivery
period as stated in the contract upon evaluation that the grounds for
extension are meritorious; (ii) take appropriate action to operate and
utilize the procured equipment for common service facility for engineered
bamboo furniture and handicrafts as intended in accordance with the
MOA between the Municipality and DOST; and (iii) henceforth, through
concerned officials, ensure compliance with the guidelines and/or
memorandum of agreement covering the utilization of the funds received
from DILG, DBM and other NGAs, if any;
d. Municipal Accountant evaluate whether the subject procured equipment
for Project 3 properly qualify for recognition in the books of the
Municipality through proper coordination with the DOST and draw
necessary adjustments, if necessary;
e. Technical Working Group carefully examine the documents submitted by
the Lowest Calculated Bid/Highest Rated Bidder during post-
qualification in compliance to Section 34.3 of the Revised IRR of RA
9184; and
f. Municipal Engineer, Municipal Accountant and other concerned offices
submit the lacking documents noted above.

We recommended that the Management (a) assign a Municipal staff to
monitor the submission of the reports of the beneficiaries and implementation of
the project and henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions stipulated in the
Tripartite Memorandum of Agreements; and (b) consider revising the MOA to
NGO/PO to include all the applicable terms of reference enumerated in Item
4.5.3 of the COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007.

We recommended that the Municipality submit justification in granting
the amount of ₱250,000.00 to K3M and not to the qualified individuals in the
informal sector and small entrepreneurs, especially those affected by the on-
going pandemic due to COVID-19, as approved by NAPC. We further
recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the concerned office,
implement similar projects on time based on the approved plan/project
proposal.

We recommended that the:
a. Local School Board (i) ensure that the SEF is appropriated and disbursed
only for the priority/allowable expenses chargeable against the SEF
specifically laid down in DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1 s. 2017, JC No. 1 s.
2020 and JC No. 2 s. 2020; (ii) prepare the Annual SEF Budget using the
prescribed format pursuant to JC No. 1 s. 2017; (iii) consider the
inclusion in the SEF budget the funding of programs, projects and
activities that would support the alternative learning modalities, digital
education, digital infrastructure, and continuity plans of the schools
within the Municipality amidst the COVID-19 pandemic, and (iv) ensure
optimal utilization of the fund;
b. BAC comply with rules and procedures under the Revised IRR of RA No.
9184 and Government Procurement Policy Board guidelines specifically
with regard to posting requirements and conduct of post-qualification,
among others; and
c. Municipal Mayor to refrain from granting extension exceeding the initial
delivery period as stated in the original contract and determine that the
extension is based on meritorious grounds.

Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor,
through the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to:
a. strictly monitor officers and employees with outstanding cash advances
and refrain from granting additional cash advance to an employee prior
to settlement of his/her previous cash advances;
b. consider designating other bonded employees as Special Disbursing
Officers to cash advance for special purpose/undertaking, whenever
necessary, to avoid overlapping cash advances; and
c. ensure that liquidation of the accountable officers are within the
reglementary period in compliance to the prescribed guidelines under
COA Circular Nos. 97-002, 2012-001 and 2012-004, as applicable.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through concerned Offices,
revisit the MOA with the ORMECO, and consider revising the terms stipulated
thereto to include mechanisms to recoup the cost of the project shouldered by
the Municipality.

We recommended that the GFPS and other offices concerned:
a. prioritize the attendance of the GFPS Executive Committee, TWG and
MET to various GAD-related trainings and seminars particularly GAD
Planning and Budgeting and Gender Analysis in order for them to serve
as a catalyst in gender mainstreaming in the Municipality and apply the
GAD tools such as HGDG, GMEF and other relevant tools in assessing
the existing programs for the preparation of the GPB and GAD AR;
b. ensure that, in the preparation of GPB (i) statement of gender issues
properly reflect the baseline data or information from sex-disaggregated
data and gender analysis results, (ii) GAD objectives are SMART
(specific, measurable, attainable, realistic and time-bound), reflect the
results that the Municipality intends to achieve, and not articulated as an
activity statement; and (iii) it conforms with the format prescribed in
PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016 showing
separately the client-focused, organization-focused and attributed
programs;
c. consistently observe the guide in completing the GAD AR prescribed
under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 2016-01;
d. exhaust all available resources for the collection and processing of
complete and reliable data requirements of the GAD Database to be used
in data processing and gender analysis for gender-responsive planning
and budgeting;
e. include priority gender issues in the succeeding GPB that may be
identified through analysis of the information in the GAD database as
well as the corresponding PPAs that will address the same;
f. prepare the GAD Agenda through proper consultations with local
functionaries and stakeholders to ensure that priority gender issues will
be addressed within the timeframe; and
g. facilitate the preparation of the IRR of GAD Code in accordance with
Section 72 of the Municipal Ordinance No. 3 s. 2016.

We commended the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer for their prompt remittance of the contributions and loan
amortizations due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth in accordance with the
pertinent provisions of law relevant. On the other hand, we recommended that
both offices exert efforts to ascertain the details of the long outstanding balance
in the Due to GSIS account for necessary adjustment, if warranted.

We recommended    that the (a) Municipal Engineer (i) inspect and evaluate the concreted road projects, and assess if the contractor may be held liable for any defects in the construction projects; and (ii) submit the certificates of completion and acceptance for the completed projects of the LGU; (b) Municipal Accountant submit to the Audit Team the SWA, photo documentation, and certificates of inspection, completion and acceptance for the completed projects; and (c) Concerned officials re-evaluate the personnel compliments of the Engineering Office to determine whether additional manpower may be employed, subject to budgetary requirements and other existing policy guidelines relative thereto.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineering Office (MEO) inspected and evaluated the projects and asserted that (1)the working days indicated in the Program of Works did not include the curing period; (2) project duration falls on rainy seasons and holidays which the labors did not work; and (3) MEO prepared the SWA based on the billings made by the contractor and not the value of works actually in place. The Municipal Accountant submitted the documents and admitted the documents were inadvertently omitted. Moreover, the contractor instituted corrective actions on the minor deficiencies noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: BAC Secretariat and Municipal Accountant submit the lacking supporting documents to support the propriety and validity of the disbursement; Municipal Accountant and Treasurer ensure that claims for the future transactions were supported with necessary documents before payments were made. Municipal Engineer and Accountant compute for the actual amount of liquidated damages that should be demanded from the contractor. Moreover, scrutinize the information indicated in the documents particularly the period or dates being prescribed; Concerned officials and employees provide satisfactory explanation for the noted observations and enforce the performance of the repair of the subjected portion of the projects; and GIST Coordinator maintains an organized inventory of equipment, supplies and materials for the efficient management of the same.
observations.managementAction: The MAcco explained that the contract duration indicated in the Program of Works (POWs) were erroneous because the MEO failed to consider construction conditions such as curing period and climate. The MEO admitted that they prepared the SWA based on the billings made by the contractor and not the value of works actually in place. The MAcco asserted that there was no actual delay as the dated indicated in the POWs are erroneous. The MAcco and BAC submitted the documents and admitted the documents were inadvertently omitted.
observations.reason: Standard contract duration asserted by the Municipal Engineer on the subject projects was not supported with any documents to substantiate the assertion which could be in the form of duly signed contracts between the Municipality and the respective contractors.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: require the Municipal Engineer to submit satisfactory explanation for the noted differences between the reported and verified accomplishments for the projects; make a formal demand against the A. S. Yang Construction for the amount of liquidated damages for the delayed completion of the projects; require the contractors A. S. Yang Construction and 811 Trading and Construction for the amount of liquidated damages brought by the delayed completion of the projects - Construction of Gloria MDRRMC Operation Center and Rural Health Unit; and require the contractor to institute corrective measures or refund the amount of cost deficiency as may be determined.
observations.managementAction: The contractor already made corrective actions to the Concreting of Municipal Cemetery road and Construction of Drainage. The liquidated damages for the Construction of Gloria MDRRMC Operation Center and Rural Health Unit were still for collection from respective contractors. A.S. Yang Construction settled the cost deficiency amounting ₱23,420.00 for the Construction of Gloria MDRRMC Operation Center.
observations.reason: Corrective actions were sent to COA Region IV-B Technical Audit Services (TAS) for reinspection and revaluation. Liquidated damages were still not collected by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Local Administration devise plans and strategies to facilitate effective management of market operations which may include: creation of a Market Development Plan for the proper organization and classification of market sections; issuance of demand letters to stallholders to encourage the timely payment of rental dues and avoidance of penalties; and issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Eviction to lessees who habitually and/or continuously violate the regulations set forth by the Revenue Code; and Market Administrator and other concerned Officials and Employees continue issuing demand letters for the collection of rental fees on due dates and encourage other lessees/stallholders to also pay their rental fees on due dates required by the regulations and agreed in the perfected Contract of Lease; Market Administrator prepare and submit Monthly Report of Collection for the proper performance monitoring and recording of receipts; and Municipal Accountant establish the appropriate receivable account to recognize the amount of accumulated uncollected goodwill and stall rentals and disclose the nature of the same in the Notes to Financial Statement for fair presentation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor and Market Inspector/Administrator served demand letters on August 15, 2019, September 18, 2019, and October 16, 2019 to enforce collection of the unpaid goodwill and delinquent rental fees with the corresponding surcharges.
observations.reason: Failure to enforce other remedies for collection such as issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Eviction to lessees who habitually and/or continuously violate the regulations set forth by the Revenue Code. Moreover, during the earlier years of implementation on of the Revenue Code, collection of goodwill was relaxed, thus the uncollected goodwill amassed until the later years without being collected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: MPDC spearhead the updating and maintenance of GAD Database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; MSWDO/GFPS observe the scheduled GAD Planning and Budgeting as provided by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-001 dated January 12, 2016 to facilitate timely submission of GPB to the Provincial DILG for subsequent review and endorsement; Heads of implementing offices ensure that GAD programs, projects and activities are implemented as planned to attain the GAD objectives; GFPS and GAD-TWG [i] convene during the GAD planning and budgeting process to facilitate gender mainstreaming in the LGU, [ii] gradually increase the gender-responsiveness of LGU programs and projects through attribution using the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) tool; [iii] attend trainings and workshops for capacity development on GAD being administered by the PCW or DILG; and [iv] strictly comply with the provisions of Section 36 of RA No. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-001 to influence PPAs of the LGU toward gender responsiveness.
observations.managementAction: Current GAD database was updated as to demography, education, health and social subsectors.
observations.reason: GFPS members and officials failed to undergo capacity building programs on GAD.

observations.recommendation: We recommended    that the Municipal Accountant review the validity of the long  outstanding payables, settle those with valid claims, and revert to unappropriated surplus of the General and Special Education Funds those without valid/legal claims.
observations.managementAction: MAcco has already reviewed and appropriately adjusted those concerned accounts payables. Out of the ₱181,977.89 payables involved, ₱71,500.00 remains representing claims from Rochel Marzo Gerance Brokerage & Appraisal Services for the ongoing process under contract of titling certain municipal lots through the Municipal Assessor’s Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: BAC Secretariat, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant submit the lacking supporting documents to support the propriety and validity of the disbursement made. BAC, TWG and BAC Secretariat submit satisfactory explanation on the noted observations in the procurement process, as well as to properly and consistently impose retention money and warranty securities; Members of the Inspection Committee and GSO, BAC & TWG, properly accomplished all the supporting documents, such as PR, NOA, NTP, PO, IAR, as well as to ensure documents received from suppliers, such as OR, SI and delivery receipts were properly dated for the efficient and effective evaluation of the same; Municipal Accountant and Treasurer ensure that future claims be supported with complete documentation as required by Section 4 of P.D. 1445 and RA No. 9184; and Municipal Budget Officer continue the proper monitoring of the budget allocation for the PPAs to aid the MDC in the continuous reevaluation of unimplemented projects, as well as the savings from completed projects to be used for other developmental projects.
observations.managementAction: The concerned Municipal officials submitted the lacking supporting documents.
observations.reason: The lacking supporting documents are subject to further evaluation by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Municipal Mayor make a formal demand against the contractors and suppliers for the amount of liquidated damages brought by the delay in completion of various projects and the delivery of goods for the implementation of various PPAs, and impose appropriate sanctions as may be deemed necessary; Municipal Engineer submit satisfactory explanation on the noted observations in the project implementation of various PPAs and properly monitor implementation of PPAs to ensure compliance and aid in accomplishment/completion of projects within timeframe stipulated, to be able to immediately deliver the needed services to the intended beneficiaries; Municipal Accountant and Treasurer monitor the payments to be made by the contractors/suppliers and all other sanctions/penalties that may be imposed by the LGU to aid in proper accounting and collections of the same.
observations.managementAction: Ongoing verification with regards to the appropriateness of imposing liquidated damages to the contractors.
observations.reason: According to the MAcco, the contract duration indicated in the Program of Works (POWs) were erroneous because the MEO failed to consider construction conditions such as curing period and climate. The MEO admitted that they prepared the SWA based on the billings made by the contractor and not the value of works actually in place.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the Municipality by educating their respective constituents through Information Education Campaign (IEC) on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes and the importance of their participation in the implementation of SWM programs and policies particularly those barangays who have not yet establish MRF and was not yet included in the group of barangays being collected with residual wastes; through the MENRO, comply with the requirements of RA No. 9003 and ensure that the existing MRF in 11 barangays are functional and the purpose of establishment of MRF is being served and provide the necessary support in enjoining the remaining 16 barangays in establishing their own MRF; make necessary follow-up with concerned officials of Department of Agriculture (DA) for the immediate release of the requested unit of shredder which will be used to shred biodegradable wastes; and see to it that the operation of Dumpsite complied with Section 2 of Rule XIII of IRR of RA No. 9003 and continue developing strategies to improve the existing Disposal Facility until the proposed Sanitary Landfill becomes operational.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor encouraged participation of Municipal Offices, Barangays, schools, and commercial establishments in institutionalizing SWM agenda through implementation of the (i) “Search for Cleanest, Greenest, Healthiest and Most Resilient Barangays and Schools” program and (ii) “Search for Top 10 Business Establishments with Environment-Friendly Practices” program. Continuous enforcement of (i) “Hiwalay kung Hiwalay” project, a waste segregation at source program and (ii) "No segregation, no collection" policy was also being done. All twenty-seven (27) Barangays of the Municipality has established Barangay SWM Council and MRFs as required by RA No. 9003.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Market Administrator and Municipal Treasurer continue issuing demand letters for the collection of rental fees on due dates and encourage other lessees/stallholders to also pay their rental fees on due dates required by the regulations and agreed in the perfected Contract of Lease; Market Administrator prepare and submit Monthly Report of Collection for the proper performance monitoring and recording of receipts; and Municipal Accountant establish the appropriate receivable account to recognize the amount of accumulated uncollected goodwill and stall rentals and disclose the nature of the same in the Notes to Financial Statement for fair presentation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor and Market Inspector/Administrator served demand letters on August 15, 2019, September 18, 2019, and October 16, 2019 to enforce collection of the unpaid goodwill and delinquent rental fees with the corresponding surcharges.
observations.reason: Failure to enforce other remedies for collection such as issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Eviction to lessees who habitually and/or continuously violate the regulations set forth by the Revenue Code. Moreover, during the earlier years of implementation of the Revenue Code, collection of goodwill was relaxed, thus the uncollected goodwill amassed until the later years without being collected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Agriculturist submit the lacking documents within five days to support the propriety and validity of the disbursement made and ensure that the same claims in the future be supported with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: The DOLE Oriental Mindoro Field Office submitted a letter dated March 13,2019 to the audit team informing the same that they are accepting liquidation of documents for cash advances of worker’s association with financial assistance granted provided the said documents has been submitted first to COA Offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: Ms. Melogean S. Candelario provide for the complete necessary documents relative to her requests for relief from accountability inclusive of a strong and valid justification for her delayed application in compliance with the provisions of COA Memorandum No. 92-751 and Sec. 499 of GAAM Vol. I, in order to ensure or facilitate the evaluation and resolution of applications; In case of noncompliance by the MSWDO, the Municipal Mayor, Accountant and Treasurer enforce immediate and full refund of the book value of the property subject of the request for relief, to be determined by the Municipal Accountant; In case of non-settlement, the Municipal Mayor, Property Officer, Accountant, and Treasurer ensure that no clearance would be granted to the MSWDO until full settlement of her accountability; and The Municipal Accountant and Property Officer make pertinent adjustments on their records to reflect the loss of the IT equipment. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant discloses the matter on the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The matter has been disclosed in the notes to the financial statements.
observations.reason: Justification on the delayed application for relief were not submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the (a) Municipal Engineer (i) inspect and
evaluate the concreted road projects, and assess if the contractor may be held
liable for any defects in the construction projects made; and (ii) submit the
certificates of completion and acceptance for the enlisted completed projects of
the LGU; (b) Municipal Accountant submit to the Audit Team the SWA, photo
documentation, and certificates of inspection, completion and acceptance for the
completed projects certified by the Municipal Engineer and the Inspectorate
Committee, since LGU has the responsibility to monitor and evaluate the overall
implementation of the project; and (c) Concerned officials re-evaluate the
personnel compliments of the Engineering Office to determine whether
additional manpower may be employed, subject to budgetary requirements and
other existing policy guidelines relative thereto.

Management comment
In response to the AOM issued, the Municipal Engineer commented that they will
re-inspect and evaluate the projects to assess if the contractor may be held liable for any
defect in the construction. Moreover, the MACO will submit the lacking documents and
will ensure that future claims will be supported with complete documentations.

Auditor’s Rejoinder
No comment was received from the Municipal Engineer relative to the projects
which are implemented thru “by-administration” which were accomplished beyond the
targeted days to complete.

We recommended that the:
• BAC Secretariat and Municipal Accountant submit the lacking supporting
documents to support the propriety and validity of the disbursement;
• Municipal Accountant and Treasurer ensure that claims for the future
transactions were supported with necessary documents before payments
were made.
• Municipal Engineer and Accountant compute for the actual amount of
liquidated damages that should be demanded from the contractor. Moreover,
scrutinize the information indicated in the documents particularly the period
or dates being prescribed;
• Concerned officials and employees provide satisfactory explanation for the
noted observations and enforce the performance of the repair of the
subjected portion of the projects from the contractor; and
• GIST Coordinator maintains an organized inventory of equipment, supplies
and materials for the efficient management of the same.

Management comment
In their response, the members of the BAC, MACO, MTO will look
thoroughly on the documentation of the projects in order to identify the lacking
documents and will immediately submit the lacking supporting documents to
ascertain the validity of disbursements. Moreover, MEO will re-inspect and evaluate
the projects to verify if the contractor may be held liable for any defects in the
construction and to demand liquidated damages, if applicable. Likewise, the GIST
Coordinator will maintain an organized inventory of equipment, supplies and
materials for efficient management by: 1) proper positioning of equipment; 2) putting
proper labels/names on the tools displayed; and 3) maintain proper inventory of the in
and out of tools through logbook etc.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
➢ require the Municipal Engineer to submit satisfactory explanation for the
noted differences between the reported and verified accomplishments for
the projects Concreting of Municipal Cemetery road and Construction of
MDRRMC Operation Center;
➢ make a formal demand against the A. S. Yang Construction for the
performance of corrective measures or for the refund of cost deficiencies
attributed to the existence of longitudinal/transverse cracks and 0.5-
meter reduction in height of second floor from floor line up to the top of the
roof beam totaling ₱520,810.03 and

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal's  Board through MENRO monitor and evaluate the implementation of the programs and policies, consider  the possibility of procuring land for sanitary landfill, and stop the practice of open dumping in the Municipality instead develop strategies on how to  improve the existing disposal facility to reduce adverse impacts on health and environment. observations.managementAction: The existing controlled dumpsite at Brgy. Manguyang was rehabilitated on February and September 2017, respectively. Waste segregation and collection is also being practiced, although not yet 100% compliant.  Barangay eco-aides were also assigned to maintain cleanliness and oversee segregated collection of wastes in barangay MRFs.
observations.reason: Failure to meet the requirements in operating dumpsite and non-establishment of MRFs in 16 out of 27 barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned Municipal government officials employees optimally utilized the fund by trying their best to locate/or suitable land as dumpsite and observe the criteria for sanitary landfill as required by Section 40 of RA 9003.

observations.recommendation:

a policy guideline setting the salary rates for the JO personnel must be established to properly compensate the services they have provided.
In his letter reply, the HRMO cited that they already drafted a policy titled Selection Standards and Hiring Rates for Job Orders and Contract of Service of Workers of the Local Government Unit of Gloria. He also mentioned that they are going to forward the same to the Sangguniang Bayan for the creation of Ordinance and proper implementation by incorporating it to the 2018 AIP. The necessity of hiring JOs according to him is now being scrutinized in the hearing of budget preparation of every office/department.

We recommended that the concerned Municipal government consider the option of adopting the automation of payroll system for the payment of wages of JO personnel and if possible, designate permanent employee that will handle the preparation of payrolls of the permanent employees and JO personnel. We also recommended the review of volume and amount of transactions drawn thru cash advances by DO-Designate and see to it that the same shall not exceed the Maximum Cash Accountability authorized under her approved fidelity bond.
The HRMO cited in his letter-reply that his Office has ongoing coordination with the Land Bank of the Philippines for the automated disbursement of job order payroll. He also mentioned that they already informed the JO employees regarding the issue and all the requirements necessary to facilitate the process.
In her response to AOM, the Municipal Treasurer mentioned that they will coordinate and encourage the Local Chief Executive for the adoption of automated payroll system for the payment of wages of JO personnel. She also mentioned that they will process for the additional fidelity bond of the DO-Designate to ensure that the CAs granted does not exceed the Maximum Cash Accountability.

We recommended that the GSO mark the service vehicles with “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY” pursuant to the requirements of the regulations to avoid possible use of government vehicles other than their official functions.

We recommended that:
Each Head of the Requisitioning Offices establish proper control over the utilization of fuel, oil and lubricants thru strict compliance with the required reports set for by the above-cited regulations; and
The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer ensure completeness of the required documentation and reporting by the Requisitioning Offices before any payment can be made to avoid audit suspension and/or disallowance.
The Municipal Accountant in his letter-reply assured the completeness of supporting documents before making any payments to avoid audit suspension and/or disallowances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer with the assistance of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to prepare and submit promptly the monthly monitoring report of the awarded infra projects and the List of ongoing PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 for audit and validation purposes and for timely preparation of Quarterly Report of the Publicized Government Programs/Projects/Activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Development Council with the assistance of the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) reassess the necessity of pursuing the unimplemented projects, otherwise reprogram the same together with the allotments for projects that are not among the items allowed under DBM and DILG JMC No. 2011-1 to  more needed developmental projects. The Council should also properly plan and prioritize all activities to be implemented during the year.

We likewise recommended that the MPDO submit justification within five days for the utilization of funds amounting to ₱431,605.50 for the items disallowed by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan and by JMC No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We also reiterated that the Municipal Development Council and Budget Officer appropriate sufficient fund for the complete implementation of the identified projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Development Council agreed on realignment of some projects and the MPDO has submitted justification relative the utilization of funds for the items disallowed by SP during the review of budget and by JMC No. 2011-1.
observations.reason: Some of the projects were still not implemented by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from certifying the availability of appropriation unless there is sufficient appropriation for a particular undertaking and ensure that the appropriation used in adjusting the fund transfer came from the budget/appropriation intended for local counterpart on KALAHI projects.
observations.managementAction: Additional appropriation for the said project has been allocated.
Liquidation periods are being observed by AOs. There were minimal cash advances that remained unsettled as compared with prior years.
observations.reason: There were still cash advances liquidated beyond prescribed period due to delayed submission of documents. Moreover, immediate refund of CAs for cancelled trips were not made by employees upon termination of intended travels.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

the Accountable Officers ensure that their cash advance/s are properly accounted and immediately and fully settled within the period stated under Section 1 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 and Section 5.1.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 or as soon as the purpose/s for which the same were granted were served;

the Municipal Accountant and the Disbursing Officer coordinate with each other for the immediate settlement of long outstanding cash advance amounting to ₱100,000.00; and

the Municipal Accountant ensure that no additional cash advance will be granted to official or employee unless the  previous one is first settled, and make pertinent disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements relative to lost money due to robbery for proper presentation.
observations.managementAction: The long outstanding cash advance amounting to ₱100,000.00 was refunded  per OR#8208446 dated Dec. 9, 2016 and deposited on Dec. 29, 2016. Disclosure relative to the lost money was included in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.reason: The remaining balance of the cash advance pertaining to robbery incident remained unliquidated as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepares the disbursement voucher for the return of the unutilized balances under the Due to Other NGAs account amounting to ₱71,153.25 and the Municipal Treasurer remits the fund to the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: The unutilized balances under Due to Other NGAs amounting to ₱71,153.25 was refunded to the Bureau of Treasury (BOT) thru Check#353973 dated June 27, 2016 and deposited on July 5, 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer submits the approved Revised Program of Works (POW) or Change/Variation Order and justification for the inclusion of and changes of rates of some workers not considered in the original approved POW, otherwise, the Municipal Treasurer enforce the refund of the overpaid labor cost amounting to ₱11,650.00. 
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Engineer submit the lacking documents to prove the propriety and validity of the labor costs paid and ensure that the same claims in the future be supported with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Engineer submitted the necessary documents and justification for the inclusion and changes of rates of some workers not considered in the original approved POW.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Agriculturist submit the lacking documents within five days to support the propriety and validity of the disbursement  made and ensure that the same claims in the future be supported with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Agriculturist submitted some of the required documents.
observations.reason: The Municipal Agriculturist failed to secure and submit certification from the DOLE that the necessary requirements were submitted and verified by their office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the MDRRMO ensure that issuances of relief goods and other relief operations are properly documented, and the GSO through the help of the MDRRMO conduct the required inventory of items and prepare corresponding reports to ascertain the balances of the relief goods that remained on hand as of year-end to reflect the correct amount of inventory. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the receipt and utilization of LDRRMF to present fairly the expense and inventory accounts in the Financial Statements

We also recommended that the officials and employees concerned in the Accounting and Treasury departments scrutinize the claims and see to it that the required supporting documents were attached before approving the claims against the government funds.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant has already corrected the erroneous recording of the expenses procured out of the LDRRMF.
Issuance of relief goods to affected individuals is now being properly documented. Inventory of relief goods is likewise being practiced by the MDRRMO to ascertain the balances of the goods that remained on hand as of year-end.
The Municipal Accounting Office has applied diligent procedures on scrutinizing and ensuring that supporting documents are complete and valid for all disbursement transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer through the help of the MDRRMO limit the charging of expenses to those PPAs included in the thematic areas of DRRM. We also recommended that the Municipal’s DRRM Council observe the regulations in the proper utilization of DRRM Fund to strengthen the disaster risk reduction and management system of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The erroneous charging was already adjusted and the correct charging of expenses against the MDRRMF is now being observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Accountant refrain from authorizing payments of claims on reimbursement basis and allow only those that are emergency in nature. Moreover, if it is impractical to pay thru checks, require the Disbursing Officer to make payments thru cash advance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has prepared and endorsed the Policies and Procedures on Handling Petty Cash Fund to the Local Finance Committee and submitted to the LCE for approval to address the issues of paying claims thru reimbursement.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that  the Municipal’s SWM Board revisit the SWM Plan and revise it by incorporating all the requirements prescribed in Section 17 of RA No. 9003 and also consider the other requirements of the NSWMC, and submit the same to the NSWMC for final approval.

We also recommended that the Municipal’s SWM Board convene regularly to monitor and review the targets and strategies to ensure efficient management of the Municipality’s SWM program.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal government was able to submit the SWM Plan before the deadline set by the NSWMC. The Commission has passed Resolution No. 586, series of 2016 on May 16, 2016 approving the 10-Year SWM Plan subject to the submission of additional required information.
observations.reason: The MSWMB failed to convene to work on the additional requirements. The MENRO is making a draft of some of the required details.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal’s SWM Board in coordination with the Municipal Health Officer and other agencies concerned prioritize the schedule for the necessary training for the SWM staff to ensure that the solid wastes are handled properly to minimize the risk of hazards to the SWM personnel and the public as well.  

We also recommended that the Municipal’s SWM Board seek for additional fund which will be used in the implementation of the proper collection, transportation and handling of solid wastes required by the provisions of the IRR of RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Training on Proper Collection, Handling and Transport of Wastes for SWM staff was conducted by the MHO and MENRO on May 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal’s SWM Board monitor and evaluate the implementation of the SWM program and see to it that the requirements for the operations of the controlled dumpsite was observed to ensure its continuous operation. 

We also recommended that the officials and employees concerned make necessary actions relative to the findings of the DENR and complied with their requirements to prevent the issuance of Notice to Sue against the LGU.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality appropriated ₱1M for the acquisition of landfill.
A letter-reply to prevent issuance of Notice to Sue against the Municipality was being sent to the DENR.
observations.reason: The Municipality continues to operate/controlled dumpsite in Brgy. Manguyang because they are still looking for suitable land to be used as dumpsite. However, their letter-reply to the DENR relative to the status of compliance on the ESWM parameters was being evaluated and subsequently forwarded to the NSWMC for approval.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendation that in the preparation of the Annual Budget the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources and Municipal Budget Officers correctly classify into Environment/Sanitary Services account the appropriation for the wages of the SWM staff. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should record the cost of those expenses incurred to appropriate expense accounts to avoid misclassification of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO and MBO has prepared the 2016 budget for SWM accordingly. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant recorded the cost of the wages of those SWM personnel to Environment/Sanitary Services account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

the Municipal Accountant and the rest of the members of the Joint Committee of the CNA submit the documents supporting the payment of CNA Incentive as provided in Items 4 and 5 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2015-2 dated November 23, 2015 and Item 5.16 of the Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, and valid justification for the immediate payment of CNA Incentive after the approval and signing of CNA, and

in case of non-compliance to the above mentioned recommendation, the Municipal Mayor, Accountant and Treasurer enforce the refund of the CNA Incentive received by the 86 municipal employees totaling ₱1,376,000.00.
observations.managementAction: The President of the MARFEG has submitted the necessary documents to support the payment of CNA Incentive.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive through the help of Human Resources and Management Officer create a committee that will formulate the guidelines on the hiring of JO and COS personnel for the proper recruitment and selection to meet the requirements of the position. Moreover, require the Department Head where those JOs and COs are assigned to submit written justification for the necessity of hiring them and maximize the services of those personnel holding permanent positions to do regular, administrative and clerical functions.

We also recommended that a policy guideline setting the salary rates for the JO and COS personnel must be established to properly compensate the services they have provided.
observations.managementAction: Crafting of policies as well as the creation of committees regarding hiring and salary of job order personnel is in  process.
observations.reason: There were certain policy formulation in different council meetings, however, the consolidation of the recommended policies and guidelines are still for finalization.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

the HRMO properly and promptly update the employee records of the Municipality, submit the necessary documents such as ARA and ERF, and reconcile with the GSIS records to ensure accurate billing and payment of employees premium contributions and loan dues with the GSIS, and

the HR Personnel and AAO strictly comply with the regulations provided in Sections 5(c) and 6(a) of R.A. 8291 and GSIS MC No. 3, s. 2012.
observations.managementAction: The reconciliation process with the GSIS records to ensure accurate billing and payment of employees’ contributions and loan payments is in process. The HRMO and AAO, with the help of the Accounting Office, are closely working with each other for the updating of membership records.
observations.reason:
Some of the GSIS members requested for deferment/
postponement of payment of their loans.

observations.managementAction: Some of the necessary documents relative to the request of relief from accountability were already submitted for evaluation. The matter has been disclosed in the Notes to FS.
observations.reason: Documentary requirements like Final Investigation Report on the stolen property was not yet given by the PNP.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 

require the municipal officials and employees to comply with the findings of the Provincial Finance Committee on PS limitation and ensure that the appropriation and expenditures for Personal Services are within the 45% PS limitation as mandated by Section 325(a) of RA 7160 and DBM LBC 98 dated October 14, 2011; and

require the municipal officials and employees to refund the payment of additional benefits that caused the excess in PS limitation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal officials and employees, who are resigned, retired and end of term personnel have refunded their share on excess PS limitation thru deduction on their terminal leave benefits.
observations.reason: The remaining and existing officials and employees were not yet making a refund of their share on excess PS limitation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator consider the available equipment of the Municipality and/or have contract with other government agencies with available equipment necessary in the projects before resorting to the implementation of the projects “by administration” and if possible refrain from renting to private contractors.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality already acquired heavy equipment that can be used for the implementation of municipal projects. Requests to the Provincial Engineering Office were arranged for the equipment which are not available in the municipality before renting from private contractors.
observations.reason: There were still equipment rented from private contractors for the implementation of various projects.
